content.text: Welcome to the Contoso Retail team! This onboarding guide will walk you through everything you need to know to get started successfully. From your first day checklist to your 90-day milestones, this document is your roadmap to becoming a confident, productive member of our warehouse team.
On your first day, you should complete the following tasks with the help of your supervisor or onboarding buddy:

items: Check in with HR at the office (Zone G) to complete final new-hire paperwork (I-9, direct deposit, tax forms).
Receive your Contoso Retail employee badge from the security desk. Badge must be worn visibly at all times on the facility floor.
Complete the 2-hour New Employee Safety Orientation with the Safety Coordinator. This is mandatory before any floor assignment.
Receive your PPE kit: hi-vis vest, safety glasses, gloves. Review the PPE Policy document.
Tour the facility with your buddy — identify all emergency exits, first aid stations, AED locations, and fire extinguishers.
Set up your WMS account login with help from your supervisor (IT Help Desk: ext. 5000).
Download the UKG Workforce app on your personal device and log in to view your schedule.
Attend the shift huddle at the start of your assigned shift.
Complete the Hazard Communication (HazCom) training module in the online learning system (Contoso Learn).

content.text: Your employee badge grants access to the facility and serves as your identifier on the floor. Important rules:

items: Your badge must be visible (clipped to your vest or shirt at chest level) at all times while in the facility.
Never allow another person to use your badge to access the facility.
If your badge is lost or stolen, report it immediately to your supervisor and the security desk.
Badge replacement fee: $15 (deducted from next paycheck after the second replacement).
WMS access is role-based. Do not attempt to access areas of WMS outside your assigned role.
System passwords must not be shared. You are responsible for all transactions made under your login.

content.text: All new team members must complete the following training within their first 30 days. Your supervisor will track your completion status:
Your supervisor will conduct milestone check-ins at 30, 60, and 90 days to review your progress, provide feedback, and confirm your standing with the company:

rows: Training completion, safety awareness, attendance, basic task proficiency
Corrective coaching plan; extended support from buddy
KPI performance (UPH, accuracy), teamwork, policy adherence
Performance Improvement Plan (PIP) if metrics not progressing
Full productivity target, independence, consideration for Lead eligibility
Termination review if KPIs and conduct standards not met

content.text: At 90 days, you transition from probationary status to regular employment. Benefits enrollment opens the first day of the month following your 90-day mark.
Every new team member is paired with an experienced Buddy for their first 30 days. Your Buddy is your go-to resource for day-to-day questions, zone navigation, and WMS tips.

items: Buddies are experienced team members who have volunteered and been trained for the role.
Your Buddy will work alongside you during your first week.
Buddies receive a $50 bonus upon your successful completion of the 30-day milestone.
If you and your Buddy have a scheduling conflict, notify your supervisor to arrange alternate support.
Feedback on the Buddy program can be submitted via the Contoso Learn portal after 30 days.
